question: Let us test the slope in the smoking and lung cancer illustrative data for statistical significance. There are 11 bivariate observations. We have established $b=0.02284$ and $S E_b=0.006976$.
answer:
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \beta=0$ versus $H_a: \beta \neq 0$
B. Test statistic. $t_{\text {stat }}=\frac{b}{S E_b}=\frac{0.02284}{0.006976}=3.27$ with $d f=n-2=11-2=9$
C. $\boldsymbol{P}$-value. $\boldsymbol{P}=0.0096$, providing good evidence against $H_0$.
D. Significance level (optional). The association is significant at $\alpha=0.01$.
